items.rationale: Sees IP as one of the largest markets in the world but rife with market failures; blockchains can create open, programmable IP layers with immutable provenance, licensing, and remixing rails — made urgent by generative AI. His single most superlative enthusiasm.
items.age_note: First stated for Story Protocol 2024-08-21; reaffirmed and broadened through 2025-06-11 and 2025-11-13, so current not stale.

items.rationale: Core thesis from The Everything Token: NFTs as ubiquitous digital assets for memorabilia, tickets, ebooks, and identity — not speculation. Believes falling L2 fees finally make low-cost digital collecting real, and wants 2026 to bring more functional use.

Argues crypto's interoperability/forwards-compatibility is the ideal — possibly only — tool for persistent portable LLM context, future-proofing vibe-coded software, agent-to-agent payment rails/guardrails, and blockchain-based IDs against AI fakes.

Pushes a rigorous framework distinguishing token types; especially keen on 'arcade tokens' (infinite-supply medium-of-exchange / rewards) as an underrated model proving neither fixed supply nor speculation is required to create value.

Views protocols (vs walled-garden platforms) as winning by embedding in many applications; ownership/portability of digital assets increases web platform competition, motivating his enthusiasm for decentralized social media.

Applies his core market-design expertise to protocol mechanics — congestion pricing, avoiding gas wars in NFT sales, and when protocols should start taking fees. Treats well-designed marketplaces, not just open markets, as the value driver.
Repeatedly argues AI will complement rather than replace labor (radiology as lead example where doomers 'got the sign wrong'); personally using LLMs to push math research papers further.
Long-standing fascination (since work with Gary Becker) with marketplaces sharing returns of medical research with patients; sees blockchain architecture solving the infrastructure/access barriers.
items.age_note: Stated once 2024-09-25 (Amino Chain) and not restated since; treat as persisted, ~15 months old.

items.rationale: His 2024 a16z 'Big Idea' was a 'renaissance of information aggregation'; engages prediction-market microstructure (herding, over-anchoring) as a live area.
items.age_note: Headline view from 2024-12-04; lightly reinforced via prediction-market commentary into mid-2025.

items.rationale: Central worry: purely speculative buying bets on market expansion while making assets harder to acquire for actual utility, distorting the ecosystem he wants built around function and identity.
Argues crypto needs clear, enforceable, congruent rules; absent them, uncertainty and inefficiency drive out legitimate actors. Explicitly rejects regulation-by-enforcement.
Warns that not all tokens are equal — company-backed tokens are not network tokens — and that fixing tokenomics up front is usually wrong; monetary policy should evolve with the ecosystem.
Sees walled-garden lock-in and, in crypto, monopolistic practices, manipulation, and fraud as threats that proper guardrails must address for fair competition.
Generative AI threatens creative work via weak attribution/permissions and floods the internet with bots/fakes, demanding image-signature, IP-provenance, and proof-of-personhood solutions.
Champions this infinite-supply, faucet-priced, redeemable model as ideal for spend-centric economies and reputation/reward programs — value without speculation.
Favors true network tokens whose value rests on decentralized network/economic foundations, sharply distinguished from company-backed tokens.

Builds all his on-chain art/puzzles on Ethereum and celebrates its 10th anniversary; credits proliferating high-throughput L2s for enabling low-cost on-chain use cases. Enthusiasm is for the rails, not price.
Explicitly flags these as not network tokens and a source of confusion that mistreats value and legal status.
Criticizes the Bitcoin-precedent habit of locking supply and emission up front; thinks it rarely fits real ecosystems whose money demand evolves.

risk_regime.rationale: Structurally bullish on crypto as technology infrastructure — IP layers, functional NFTs, AI×crypto, well-designed tokens — and personally a deep, continuous on-chain builder/collector throughout the period. But his bullishness is firmly conditioned: he is anti-speculation, pro-regulation/guardrails, and emphasizes use-value over price. So his posture is 'risk-on for building, risk-off for speculation,' not directional market timing. He issues no tactical macro calls and routinely disclaims investment advice.

notes: Corpus is ~95% personal NFT collecting, recreational number theory, Wordle, and economics/public-health/political commentary; substantive market views are a minority but highly consistent. Views are overwhelmingly STRUCTURAL — he frames everything through long-horizon market design and infrastructure, with essentially no tactical price/cycle views (he repeatedly disclaims investment advice). He does not promote specific tradeable tokens; 'tokens' here are token *classes/concepts* plus ETH as a build substrate. Strong recurring non-crypto convictions (which are not crypto-market views but color his risk lens): bearish on tariffs/protectionism, sharply critical of anti-vaccine/anti-public-health policy, and persistently worried about COVID/Long COVID — all stable across the full window. AI-as-complement and AI-for-research enthusiasm grew notably stronger through 2025 (multiple papers co-developed with LLMs by Dec 2025). No major reversals detected; the Story Protocol/IP thesis (2024-08) was reinforced rather than abandoned, and the functional-vs-speculative-NFT stance is unwavering from 2024 through 2025-12.
